entities.name: PIGEONPEA
entities.description: The crop used in the field experiments to test biopesticide efficacy.

entities.name: HELICOVERPA ARMIGERA
entities.description: The pest insect that causes damage to pigeonpea.

entities.name: BIOPESTICIDES
entities.description: Biological agents used for the control of pests.

entities.name: SPINOSAD 45 SC
entities.description: A specific biopesticide treatment found to be the most effective against H. armigera.

entities.name: CONSORTIA OF BIOPESTICIDES
entities.description: A combination of biopesticides used as a treatment.

entities.name: NEEM FRUIT POWDER
entities.description: A biopesticide derived from neem fruit used for pest control.

entities.name: HANPV
entities.description: A biopesticide (Nucleopolyhedrovirus) used against H. armigera.

entities.name: STREPTOMYCES SP. (SAI-25)
entities.description: A biological agent used in the treatment of pigeonpea.

entities.name: POD AND SEED DAMAGE
entities.description: The measure of damage caused by H. armigera to the pods and seeds of pigeonpea.

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: The agricultural productivity of pigeonpea measured in kg/ha.

entities.name: ICRISAT HYDERABAD
entities.description: The location of the experimental field where the study was conducted.

entities.name: FIELD EXPERIMENTS
entities.description: The research approach used to evaluate the efficacy of biopesticides under natural conditions.
